underlying_data_quality.note: The 17 base papers in CS_THEORY_PAPERS are REAL papers from computer science literature. However, several papers have inaccurate metadata in the seed file:

inaccuracies_found.paper: Algebraic Complexity Theory and Circuit Lower Bounds
inaccuracies_found.issue: Cannot verify 2006 Avi Wigderson paper with this exact title. Related work on algebraic complexity exists (1996, 2008) but not this exact title/year combination.
Actual paper is 'The Knowledge Complexity of Interactive Proof Systems' by Goldwasser, Micali, Rackoff (1989). Seeds list pages as 291-304 but actual pages are 186-208.

inaccuracies_found.paper: Hardness of Approximation Between P and NP
inaccuracies_found.issue: Metadata lists author as 'Boaz Barak' but actual book is by Aviad Rubinstein (2017-2021, ACM Books). Barak is not the author.
Metadata claims SIAM Journal on Computing vol 18, pages 1167-1184. Actual paper 'The cell probe complexity of dynamic data structures' was published in STOC 1989, not SIAM.

inaccuracies_found.paper: Probabilistic Algorithms for Approximate String Matching
inaccuracies_found.issue: Cannot verify 1986 publication in Theoretical Computer Science vol 68. Baeza-Yates and Gonnet work on string matching appears in 1988-1992 publications.
Cannot find 2010 paper by Chakrabarti and Khot with this exact title. Related work exists but exact match not verified.
The actual book 'Parameterized Algorithms' (2015, Springer) has 8 authors (Cygan, Fomin, Kowalik, Lokshtanov, Marx, Pilipczuk, Pilipczuk, Saurabh), not just 3.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses 20 real computer science papers (17 unique + 3 pairs of duplicates). The papers span 1958-2021 and cover algorithms, complexity theory, and cryptography. The task correctly specifies: tag='cite-in-paper' (all 20 papers tagged), 3 papers with empty journal fields (indices 11-13 in seed data), 3 duplicate pairs (indices 14-16 each inserted twice), and expected output of 17 entries after deduplication and field-filling. All metadata claims in task.json about task structure are verified. However, the underlying paper database has 7 publications with inaccurate metadata: wrong authors (Barak vs Rubinstein), wrong publication venues/pages, or unverifiable publication details.
